data.body:
# Session: Jira-sync diagnosis, 3 merged fixes, and the plan-review fan-out optimization epic (c81c)

## Merged to main this session
- PR #46 — opt-in continuous-loop cadence for the Jira reconcile bridge (repo var RECONCILE_CONTINUOUS gates self-dispatch via workflow_dispatch; */20 cron kept as backstop; variable set =true on public repo navapbc/rebar). Tracked: sat-dice-tuft (closed, signed completion verdict via a main-pinned worktree).
- PR #53 — double + centralize LLMConfig defaults: DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS 8000->16000 + DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS/DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S constants (config.py). Fixed plan-review verify truncation (finish_reason=length).
- PR #55 — plan-review observability logging: per-LLM-call (runner.py) + Pass-1 batch composition + container per-pairing timing (pass1.py), gated by REBAR_LOG_LEVEL=INFO. This made the "hang" legible.

## Jira-sync diagnosis (in_progress tickets not showing as In Progress in Jira) — DIAGNOSIS ONLY, not fixed
Root cause: the reconciler diffs against an INDEX-LAGGED ACLI JQL `workitem search` snapshot (eventually-consistent), not the immediately-consistent REST GET -> status churns (re-emits changed=[status] every pass) and REB-427 is stuck (in_progress locally, To Do in Jira) because the stale snapshot reads it as already In Progress. 20-min cron adds delay. 3/4 in_progress tickets were actually correct. Fix direction: diff status against the REST GET, not the lagged search.

## MAIN WORK: epic c81c (sulky-pane-flag) — plan-review fan-out optimization
### Root cause (PROVEN, via /joe-debug)
Container criteria G3/G4 run as 2*N_children SEQUENTIAL agentic LLM calls (pass1.py:_run_container), OUTSIDE the thread pool — N=8 => 16 calls ~27 min, looked like a hang. Proven via faulthandler stacks (blocked on network select, not spinning) + per-pairing instrumentation (pairings complete + advance at 73-146s each). Bounded-but-slow, NOT a loop.
### Provenance (PROVEN, ticket history)
NOT a workflow-migration regression and NOT a partial-re-review mechanism. Authored in the BESPOKE harness at commit 832b3af0 (story ca03/dry-frost-zenith, "ca03 AC5", 2026-06-23, ~2 days pre-migration) for CONTEXT-WINDOW FITTING with content kept whole ("NEVER CHUNK CONTENT"). Migration (ba379b75/8b888716) extracted it verbatim. Container loop has zero checkpoint/scoping; the delta-re-review feature (boil-golem-veto/grace-thud-feint) is separate and uses a tripwire probe (rejected subset-reuse). Bug promoted to this epic.
### Plan (6 children; DAG: S1->{S2,S3}; S3->S4; S6->{S4,S5}; S4->S5)
- S1 0250 — Prompt caching at the runner seam: system-prompt caching via AnthropicModelSettings(anthropic_cache_instructions+tool_definitions), ANTHROPIC-GATED; capture result.usage cache tokens. FIDELITY-NEUTRAL. STATUS in_progress (spike done; code not yet written).
- S3 ba7e — Parallelize container fan-out + WARM-THEN-FAN-OUT (warm one real pairing to completion, then fan out the rest in a NEW pool inside _run_container) + fix the centrality budget cap so container criteria are NEVER shed. FIDELITY-NEUTRAL. Depends S1.
- S2 c6e5 — Engine-wide caching via RunnerAgentStep: relocate per-run data out of 11 prompts' system body (workflow/runs.py + prompts.py); FIDELITY-VERIFY the relocated gate prompts (esp. completion-verifier that backs the close gate) via parity.py. Depends S1.
- S6 da34 — Fidelity-eval: ADD G3/G4 to the existing eval_specs suite + reuse parity.py (recall/false-accept). LIVE eval opt-in/non-blocking. READY (no blockers). relates_to 6f2d (WS-EVAL-EXISTING).
- S4 98c6 — Merge G3+G4 into one call per bin (2N->N); validate model-returned criteria against {G3,G4}; update sizing.py projection. Depends S3+S6 (eval-gated).
- S5 1762 — Child bin-packing (pack small WHOLE children to the P8 window budget; preserve single-child fallback; REQUIRED per-child output). Depends S4+S6 (eval-gated).
### De-risk experiments (ALL PASSED — recorded as ticket comments)
- E1 (S1): caching survives the REAL agentic+structured-output+tools container shape — full cache hit.
- E2 (S3): warm-then-fan-out — cold concurrent x3 = 3 writes/0 reads (the ~20x all-miss problem is REAL); warmed = 0 writes/3 reads. Parallelize MUST warm first.
- E3 (S2): 11/45 prompts interpolate per-run data into the SYSTEM body -> refactor scoped (completion_verifier/code_quality/security/tests/ticket_quality use ticket_context+ticket_id; plan_review_* use plan).
- D (S3/S4, offline): budget shed DROPS G3 at N=20, G3+G4 at N=40 (MAJOR-5 real); halving container cost (merge) pushes the threshold past N=40; "container never shed" is the robust fix.
- A (S1, integration): rebar's REAL runner caches the container fan-out end-to-end (1 write / 8 reads).
SPIKE (recorded on S1): anthropic_cache_instructions caches the Agent(system_prompt=) kwarg. Usage fields = result.usage.cache_read_tokens / cache_write_tokens (a PROPERTY, not a method). The two redesign-if-wrong risks (S1 caching, S3 warm-then-fan-out) are ELIMINATED.
### Reviews + remediation applied
- Opus epic review -> 3 design problems remediated: BLOCKER-1/2 (rescope S1 to system-prompt caching, no user-message CachePoint — spike+E1 confirmed it works for our call shape, avoids breaking the structured-output retry loop); BLOCKER-3 (new S6 SEMANTIC eval — the existing parity harness is STRUCTURAL/zero-model-call); MAJOR-4/5/6/7.
- Single-pass G3/G4 review of the epic (run as the merge+pack EXTREME, one subagent over all 6 children): found S2's relocation of 11 prompts (incl. the close-gate completion-verifier) had NO fidelity check -> added an S2 verification AC; stale S1-S5 enumeration -> fixed to S1-S6 + a sequencing note. META: single-pass IMPROVED cross-child G4 recall (caught an inter-child gap a per-child fan-out would MISS) but skewed attention toward early/most-annotated children -> the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (S5, and MUST apply to S4) is essential; S6 should specifically check TAIL-child recall.
### Eval alignment + CI posture
Existing infra: Inspect/promptfoo seam + eval_specs/*.eval.yaml per-criterion suite (built under closed epic 5fd2) + parity.py (recall/false-accept, min_gold). G3/G4 are the ONE uncovered gap -> S6 fills it; relates_to epic 6f2d WS-EVAL-EXISTING (standing parallel-run-and-diff). CI posture already correct and matches the cautions: only the OFFLINE deterministic eval-discipline check is required/blocking; the LIVE non-deterministic eval is workflow_dispatch-only (never blocks, zero auto cost). S6 keeps this; S4/S5 use the eval as a HUMAN-REVIEWED dev gate, NOT a CI blocker.
### READINESS for a fresh session — READY
All 6 children pass check-ac; file_impact set on all; DAG wired correctly. START POINTS: S6 da34 (open/ready — the eval harness, buildable now using existing infra) and S1 0250 (in_progress, spike done — implement the AnthropicModelSettings caching + usage capture in runner.py). Then S3+S2 (after S1), S4 (after S3+S6), S5 (after S4+S6). Implement strictly in DAG order: S4/S5's sizing.py cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's never-shed rule lands. Epic + S1 are in_progress (assignee blank — a fresh session continues/re-claims). All de-risk findings, the spike result, and the experiment numbers are in the ticket comments. Ephemeral experiment scripts lived in the job tmp dir (not persisted) — the FINDINGS are in the tickets.

## Key code references
runner.py (PydanticAIRunner.run — the cache seam ~line 209-246, model_settings injection point); pass1.py:_run_container (the sequential fan-out + the closed Pass-1 pool) + run_pass1; passes.py:pass1_container (builds the container RunRequest; system prompt carries the stable parent plan); sizing.py (centrality budget cap + shed projection len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT); workflow/runs.py:154-217 (RunnerAgentStep / build_agent_request); reviewers/plan_review_{G3,G4,container}.md; eval_specs/; parity.py. pydantic-ai 1.107.0: CachePoint (provider-safe no-op off-anthropic) + AnthropicModelSettings; min cache prefix 4096 tokens on Opus 4.8; read ~0.1x; warm-then-fan-out required under parallelism.
